title: clematis australis var. rutifolia
intro: Clematis australis var. rutifolia, commonly known as the Karamu, is a flowering vine native to New Zealand. It has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are said to aid in the treatment of various health issues. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and soothing respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Karamu can be used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
